title: The Craft of Intelligence
author: Allen Dulles
content: “The intelligence business is the business of getting the right information to the right place at the right time.”
action: Record: Information is useless unless it is actionable and timely.

title: Spycatcher
author: Peter Wright
content: “The hardest part of the job was not gathering the intelligence, but convincing the decision-makers to believe it.”
action: Record: The bottleneck in any system is the skepticism of leadership, not the data itself.

title: The Ultra Secret
author: F.W. Winterbotham
content: “The breaking of the Enigma code was the single most important factor in shortening the war.”
action: Record: Asymmetric information access is a force multiplier that offsets logistical inferiority.

title: The Mitrokhin Archive
author: Christopher Andrew
content: “The KGB’s success was built on the meticulous, long-term cultivation of 'assets' deep within enemy structures.”
action: Record: Long-term, low-visibility infiltration is more stable than high-impact, short-term espionage.

title: Blind Man's Bluff
author: Sherry Sontag
content: “Submarine intelligence was the most dangerous, most secret, and most vital aspect of the Cold War.”
action: Record: The most critical data is always found in the most inaccessible, high-risk environments.
